---
title: "About"
description: >-
  Biography, education, employment, grants, awards, teaching and technical
  background of Natalia Gumińska, PhD.
toc: true
---

::: {.lead .reveal}
I am a postdoctoral researcher in the **Laboratory of RNA Biology (ERA Chairs
Group)** at the International Institute of Molecular and Cell Biology in Warsaw.
I work on poly(A) tails: how long they are, what they are actually made of, and
how to measure both reliably with Oxford Nanopore direct RNA sequencing.
:::

## Biography

My background is genuinely two-sided. I hold a PhD in molecular biology from the
Faculty of Biology at the University of Warsaw and, in parallel, degrees in
Polish philology — literary studies and linguistics — from the Faculty of Polish
Studies. Reading texts closely and reading sequencing data closely turn out to
require the same kind of attention: notice the thing that does not fit, then
work out whether it is a signal or an artefact.

My early research was on euglenids: the order in which conventional and
nonconventional introns are removed, circular RNAs derived from those introns,
circular extrachromosomal DNA, and DNA barcoding for environmental samples.
Since 2020 I have worked on RNA tailing, developing methods and software for
nanopore direct RNA sequencing. To do that properly I went back to school for
data science — first R, now Python.

## Employment

::: {.timeline .reveal}
::: {.timeline-item}
<div class="timeline-date">2020 — present</div>
<div class="timeline-title">Postdoctoral Researcher</div>
<div class="timeline-org">Laboratory of RNA Biology (ERA Chairs Group), International Institute of Molecular and Cell Biology in Warsaw</div>
<p>Poly(A) tail biology and nanopore direct RNA sequencing. Method development
and research software, including ninetails and nanotail2, plus the laboratory's
sequencing metadata infrastructure.</p>
:::
:::

## Education

::: {.timeline .reveal}
::: {.timeline-item}
<div class="timeline-date">2025 — present</div>
<div class="timeline-title">Postgraduate studies, Python Data Science Engineer</div>
<div class="timeline-org">Koźminski University, Warsaw</div>
:::
::: {.timeline-item}
<div class="timeline-date">2021 — 2022</div>
<div class="timeline-title">Postgraduate studies, Data Science with R (with honours)</div>
<div class="timeline-org">Faculty of Economic Sciences, University of Warsaw</div>
:::
::: {.timeline-item}
<div class="timeline-date">2015 — 2020</div>
<div class="timeline-title">PhD in Biology, molecular biology (with honours)</div>
<div class="timeline-org">Faculty of Biology, University of Warsaw</div>
:::
::: {.timeline-item}
<div class="timeline-date">2012 — 2015</div>
<div class="timeline-title">M.Sc. in Biotechnology</div>
<div class="timeline-org">Faculty of Biology, University of Warsaw</div>
:::
::: {.timeline-item}
<div class="timeline-date">2012 — 2014</div>
<div class="timeline-title">M.Sc. in Polish Philology, literary studies and linguistics (with honours)</div>
<div class="timeline-org">Faculty of Polish Studies, University of Warsaw</div>
:::
::: {.timeline-item}
<div class="timeline-date">2010 — 2013</div>
<div class="timeline-title">B.Sc. in Biotechnology</div>
<div class="timeline-org">Faculty of Biology, University of Warsaw</div>
:::
::: {.timeline-item}
<div class="timeline-date">2009 — 2012</div>
<div class="timeline-title">B.Sc. in Polish Philology</div>
<div class="timeline-org">Faculty of Polish Studies, University of Warsaw</div>
:::
:::

## Technical expertise

A short version; the [Stack](stack.qmd) page has the full breakdown with
proficiency levels.

::: {.card-grid}
::: {.u-card .reveal .d1}
#### Method development
Signal-level analysis of nanopore reads, poly(A) tail length estimation, tail
composition classification, benchmarking against orthogonal methods.
:::

::: {.u-card .reveal .d2}
#### Research software
R package development end to end: API design, roxygen2 documentation, testthat
suites, pkgdown sites, CI on GitHub Actions.
:::

::: {.u-card .reveal .d3}
#### Data infrastructure
Relational schema design for laboratory metadata, access control, audit
logging, and reproducible export to analysis-ready formats.
:::
:::

## Research grants

- **2018 — 2021** Characteristics of circular extrachromosomal DNA in euglenids
  (Euglenida). Polish National Science Centre, Preludium 14
  (2017/27/N/NZ2/01321). *Principal investigator.*
- **2017 — 2019** Evaluation of diversity, species composition and population
  dynamics of phototrophic euglenids in environmental samples from a selected
  hypereutrophic habitat, based on the V2 18S rDNA region. University of Warsaw,
  Faculty of Biology research grant for young scientists.

## Awards and scholarships

- **2026** Prof. W. Krzyżosiak Award for the publication *Re-adenylation by
  TENT5A enhances efficacy of SARS-CoV-2 mRNA vaccines* (*Nature*, 2025), as a
  team member. Committee on Molecular Cell Biology, Polish Academy of Sciences.
- **2024** Best poster presentation, 29th Annual Meeting of the RNA Society,
  Edinburgh.
- **2023** Best poster presentation, 1st Polish RNA Meeting, Warsaw.
- **2022** Best poster presentation, 27th Annual Meeting of the RNA Society,
  Boulder.
- **2019** Rector's award for scientific achievement (team member).
- **2019** Best poster presentation, 8th European Congress of Protistology, Rome.
- **2017** Holz-Conner Travel Award, ISOP annual meeting, Prague.
- **2016** Rector's award for scientific achievement (team member).
- **2015 — 2020** Rector's scholarship for PhD students.
- **2009 — 2014** Rector's scholarship for academic performance, and Indeks
  Start2Star Scholarship.

## Teaching

- **2026** *A tail of tails: computer vision in nanopore analysis.* Lecturer,
  Advanced Path mentoring and use-case presentation, Girls Code Fun Foundation,
  Warsaw.
- **2024** *Nanopore Direct RNA Sequencing Workshop.* Lecturer, Adam Mickiewicz
  University, Excellence Initiative Research University (IDUB), Poznań.

## Community service

- **2020 — 2023** Postdoc Representative, International Institute of Molecular
  and Cell Biology.
- **2016 — 2018** Secretary of the Electoral Committee, Faculty of Biology,
  University of Warsaw.

## Courses and certificates

- **2023** Nature Masterclasses trainer-led workshop, *Getting Published:
  Effectively Communicating Your Research* (online).
- **2021** An introduction to nanopore direct RNA sequencing, Physalia, Berlin.
- **2020** Introduction to statistics in R, Physalia, Berlin.
- **2019** EMBL course, *Shift your DNA and RNA Sequencing Library Preparation
  into Hyper-Drive*, EMBL Heidelberg.
- **2018** Basic Python programming and data analysis for biologists, Nencki
  Institute, Warsaw.
- **2016** Genome Sequencing (Bioinformatics II) and Finding Hidden Messages in
  DNA (Bioinformatics I), UC San Diego; Introduction to Genomic Technologies,
  Johns Hopkins University (Coursera).

## Outside work

Digital art, tabletop and computer RPGs, fantasy, and music — rock, metal and
classical. One of those hobbies produced actual software. That, and everything
else that does not fit a CV heading, lives on the
[Side quests](side-quests.qmd) page.

::: {.btn-row .mt-3}
<a class="btn-cta" href="cv/index.qmd"><i class="bi bi-file-earmark-person"></i> Full CV</a>
<a class="btn-ghost" href="publications/index.qmd"><i class="bi bi-journal-text"></i> Publications</a>
<a class="btn-ghost" href="contact.qmd"><i class="bi bi-envelope"></i> Contact</a>
:::
